morning of the primaries.


The police are searching for MIL
ton c. Hood, II, Plaza, who has
been missing from his home for
several days and is thought to be
in Spokane


For carrying a pistol In Judge
Webster's court room, Tony Rossi,
private secretary to Frank Bruno,
was fined $50 and costs by Judge
Mann yesterday afternoon.


City Councilmen Gray and Mohr
were stalled in city auto near the
line between Spokane and Stevens
counties yesterday afternoon and
had to walk two miles to car.
In order that tho water wagons
in the North Side famine district
shall never pass without warning,
horns have been purchased by the
city for the use of the drivers.


Damages In the sum OF $25,5OO
are asked by Alphonse ]. Januick, n
switchman, from the Inland and
Northern Pacific roads because of a
permanently crippled knee.
The new ST. xavier's school in
Lidgerwood has started out with an
enrolment of nearly 20.


The Union Securities Co. Of Spo-
kane has purchased a controlling in
terest in the Farmers State bank of
Nez Perez, Idaho, the Ilo State bank
and the Sites, Idaho, bank. 1t now
controls the four principal banks of
Idaho and Nez Perez counties.


Stella Suter, in a divorce suit
against Andrew Suter, alleges that
he failed to provide for her.
Five dollars and costs were as
sessed against Mrs. Jane F. Doe
Poins by Justice Hyde because of
scrap with her neighbor, Mrs. Col
Inns, on Madelia street.


May Westbrook against w. L.
Westbrook for desertion and Laura
Mccormick against Thomas J. Nc
Cormick for nonsupport are two adj
ditional divorce cases started in the
superior court.


Michael SmithWick, aged 60, has
filed in the superior court an action
charging fraud to his sister and
brother in law, Mr.. and Mrs. E. c.
Murphy. He claims that through a
conspiracy engineered by them they
managed to cheat him out of valu-
able property.


Roy Slater, the we'll known real
estate man, says that unless the
people elect Miles Poindexter the
east side will be without a senator
after while. Mr.. Slater believes in
the truth of the report that Senator
Wesley l.. Jones proposes to re-
move TO Seattle after the coming
election.


Funeral services for Miss Edith
Bodner, who died Tuesday night at
the Deaconess hospital, were held
this afternoon at the Turnbull
capel. The burial took place at
Greenwood.


-e-Feryuson, on a plea Of guilty to
charge of grand larceny, was
given six months to Is years at
Walla Walla by Judge Hinkle.
Her husband did not like her
pancakes or other cooking, trouble
arose In consequence and then Mrs.
Effie Briggs cued Edward Briggs
for . divorce. Judge Hinkle granted
| the application.
